title: Trump’s ‘Learning Curve’ on China Ends With Conciliation at Summit
summary: The president has shifted the foundations of American policy toward China, throwing aside the adversarial approach of recent years.

title: Xi Pitches His Vision for Avoiding Conflict With the U.S.
summary: Having fought the Trump administration to a draw, China’s Xi Jinping is proposing “constructive strategic stability,” aimed at drawing lines he thinks the U.S. should not cross.

title: For Xi’s Critics, Trump’s China Visit Is Fuel for Jokes They Can’t Tell
summary: On Threads and other sites, liberal-minded Chinese accounts were mocking the proceedings and offering a rare window into opinions on Xi Jinping and his leadership style.

title: Trump Discussed U.S. Arms Sales With Xi ‘In Great Detail’
summary: The engagement between the president and the Chinese leader may have tested a decades-old U.S. assurance to Taiwan not to consult Beijing on the topic.

title: Kataib Hezbollah Commander Accused of Planning Attacks on N.Y.C.
summary: Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi is accused of leading attacks in Europe and being part of a retaliation campaign by Iran. Prosecutors say he is a leader of Kataib Hezbollah, an Iraqi militia with ties to Iran.

title: Back From China, Trump Faces Decision on Whether to Resume Strikes on Iran
summary: Top aides have drafted battle plans as peace negotiations have stalled.

title: Republicans Waited to Challenge Trump on the Iran War. Now It May Be Too Late.
summary: Having deferred to the president for months, G.O.P. lawmakers missed crucial milestones to try to limit his war powers. That has tied their hands in seeking parameters and exit criteria.

title: Executions Surge in Iran Since Cease-fire, Rights Groups Say
summary: Many were detained during January’s mass protests, according to a rights group, amid concerns the authorities are trying to intimidate Iranians from returning to the streets.

title: Judge Declares Mistrial in Weinstein Rape Trial
summary: This was the third time Harvey Weinstein was on trial accused of raping an aspiring actress at a hotel in Manhattan in 2013.

title: Justice Dept. Aims to Use Terrorism Laws to Target Mexican Officials
summary: In a new tactic, the Justice Department this week instructed federal prosecutors to build criminal drug cases against Mexican officials using terrorism statutes.

title: How a Drug Cartel Made a Mexican State Its Tool
summary: Cartel insiders say that in exchange for bribes and political support they were allowed to operate in Sinaloa state with near total freedom.

title: Archaeologists Find Egyptian Mummy Buried With the ‘Iliad’
summary: For the deceased of Roman-era Egypt, Greek literature may have offered a cheat code to a more comfortable afterlife.

title: Fierce Fights Over House Seats to Replace Nancy Pelosi and Jerrold Nadler
summary: Nancy Pelosi and Jerrold Nadler are retiring, prompting competitive races to succeed them. Whoever wins their seats could tap big donors and shape the Democratic Party.

title: Trump Loyalist Leads Administration’s Hunt for Fraud in Elections
summary: The loyalist, Dan Bishop, put his fealty to the president on display when he pressed the F.B.I. to chase investigative leads it had already rejected.

title: With Memphis District Gone, Another Democratic Mainstay Retires
summary: Representative Steve Cohen, a Democrat who has represented Memphis since 2007, leaving the field possibly to his young rival, Justin J. Pearson, after Republicans redistrict his seat.

title: Martin Short and the Secret to Finding Joy While Surviving Tragedy
summary: The comedy star, who is the subject of a new documentary, has faced a series of unimaginable losses. Yet he says there are always laughs to remember.

title: Deported Despite DACA: Dreamers Face Uncertainty Under Trump
summary: The administration has said DACA isn’t a right to stay in the United States “indefinitely.” One man with DACA was detained and deported to Mexico in a matter of days.

title: Inside the Congolese Hotel Where Trump Deported 15 U.S. Migrants
summary: They were shackled and sent to Kinshasa by the Trump administration. Now they face a dangerous choice: Go back to Latin America or stay in Africa.

title: German Chancellor, After Iran Spat With Trump, Reports ‘Good’ Phone Call
summary: The call, initiated by Friedrich Merz, came soon after he told young people, “I am a great admirer of America. My admiration is not increasing at the moment.”

title: Why a Retrial of the Murdaugh Case Could Look a Lot Different
summary: It is not clear when another trial would take place. But both prosecutors and defense lawyers said they would be forced to revise their legal strategies.

title: The Strange Alliance Trying to Remake American Psychiatry
summary: The Department of Health and critical psychiatry activists are hoping to upend 40 years of medical orthodoxy.

title: Replacing a Royal Hand-Me-Down That Finally Frayed
summary: Three years after King Charles III was crowned, the Royal Opera House in London hung plush new stage curtains bearing the monarch’s insignia — but only after the old ones gave up the ghost.

title: In Italy, Princess Catherine Puts Her Wardrobe to Work
summary: Suits her, sir.

title: Why ‘Smart’ Products Have Started to Look Like the Dumb Choice
summary: How Wi-Fi-connecting, app-based tech led to a backlash in the name of simplicity.

title: The Damage of ‘Trump Math’ Is Adding Up
summary: Authoritarians go after data. The president has already started.

title: Keir Starmer and the End of Britain’s Old Certainties
summary: The decade since Brexit has been one long, painful process of trying — and failing — to make sense of this new reality.

title: At the Summit
summary: We wrap up President Trump’s visit to China.

title: It’s a Hard Time to be a Young Adult. We Want to Hear From You.
summary: I’m a Times reporter writing about young people in the New York City area. Whether you’re a young adult, or work with them, your insights can help.

title: Stock Market Rally Confronts Inflation and Higher Interest Rates
summary: Despite accelerating inflation and possible interest rate increases, the S&P 500 has posted a long weekly winning streak, driven by strong corporate earnings. Can it last?

title: Joe Sedelmaier, Auteur Behind ‘Where’s the Beef?’ Ad, Dies at 92
summary: He directed nearly 1,000 comedic commercials, including a much-quoted spot for Wendy’s and one for FedEx featuring a manic speed talker.

title: Large Ebola Outbreak Is Declared in Congo
summary: Dozens of deaths and hundreds of infections are suspected, an African agency said. Health experts were alarmed that the outbreak hadn’t been announced sooner.
